text: The surgeon performs lateral dissection of the colon, carefully avoiding the mesenteric vessels to maintain flexibility in the surgical approach. This technique aims to enhance visualization of the colon for identifying any external features indicative of cancer.

The surgeon performs lateral mobilization of the colon, revealing severe diverticulosis in the bowel, which appears concerning for anastomosis despite the patient never having experienced diverticulitis.
The surgeon performs an intraoperative colonoscopy, navigating through the colon where large stool balls intermittently fall into the lumen, visible within the colon's dilated diverticula.
The surgeon carefully withdraws the colonoscope, adjusting the camera view to provide an endoscopic perspective of the colon's interior, demonstrating the extent of the scope's reach.
